title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Today I'm going to show you my weekend routine. It's beautiful out today so I'm heading to the beach.

Hmm, curry, crispy roll. I feel like it might be one of those things that's salty and sweet, but I like very salty snacks. So I'm going to stick with pringles, but I've never tried this flavor before.
It's Korean fried chicken pringles. Let's give it a shot. I'm going to check out the ice cream selection because a hot day, like today, calls for a nice cold treat.

It's good. Let's take a sip. How is this zero sugar? It's so sweet. This is the best protein drink I've ever had. Let's make the light. I feel like this is going to melt really fast, so I need to eat it right away. I found a little bit of shade to eat my ice cream in. I'm so curious about this.

The sand is hot. Nothing but clear skies today. I'm going to get a nice tan. I think I'm going to rent in umbrella and a tube today. When I think of beaches in Korea, this is what comes to mind. All of the umbrellas lined up on the beach. Beaches in America aren't really like this. In general, I think beaches in Korea are a bit more controlled. There's always a lifeguard on duty. The beach is closed certain times of the year. But in the US, we mostly swim at our own risk.

title: will / shall
examples: I will explain what I'm doing along the way and teach you English expressions and vocabulary that you can use in your daily life. I recommend shadowing me to practice your speaking and improve your pronunciation. First things first, let's pack a beach bag. I've got my beach day essentials in front of me. Obviously I need a swimsuit. Sometimes I wear a bikini, but today I'm packing my one piece. I don't want to get a sunburn, so I've got sunscreen and a sun stick for my face. I'm packing two beach towels and a book, so I don't get bored when I'm tanning.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: I've got my sunglasses already on my head. Now I just have to put on my sandals before I go.

I don't want to jump y'all. At least I'm getting used to the water. Usually, I like to go under, but I did my makeup for the camera today, so no dunking my head this time. When I go under, I always flood my nose and hold my breath. The buis are so far out, but let's not go too deep. Let's stay in the shallow water. This is my happy place. I'm like a mermaid.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I will explain what I'm doing along the way and teach you English expressions and vocabulary that you can use in your daily life. I recommend shadowing me to practice your speaking and improve your pronunciation. First things first, let's pack a beach bag. I've got my beach day essentials in front of me. Obviously I need a swimsuit. Sometimes I wear a bikini, but today I'm packing my one piece. I don't want to get a sunburn, so I've got sunscreen and a sun stick for my face. I'm packing two beach towels and a book, so I don't get bored when I'm tanning.
They're good, but not my favorite flavor of Pringles. It's kind of boring, but I think I like plain original Pringles the most. I lied. These are growing on me, and now I can't stop eating them.
It was nice being a beach bum, but I was getting hot and sweaty, so I changed back into my clothes, and I could use some AC, so I'm going to find a nice cafe with a good view of the beach.

Should I go complain? I guess I just have to eat this. Just kidding, guys, but I did have a subscriber ask how to complain in English, so if you ever get the wrong order, you can say something like, excuse me, I think I got the wrong order, or I'm sorry, but this isn't what I ordered.
I'm looking for seashells. Let's see if we can find any cool ones.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: Hmm, curry, crispy roll. I feel like it might be one of those things that's salty and sweet, but I like very salty snacks. So I'm going to stick with pringles, but I've never tried this flavor before.

title: have to / must
examples: I've got my sunglasses already on my head. Now I just have to put on my sandals before I go.
I have to try this. It looks like fried chicken, but it's ice cream. I hope it's not chicken flavored.

I think it's just vanilla ice cream on the inside and it's coated with cornflakes or some kind of cereal. I actually surprisingly recommend it. It's really good. This is my new favorite kind of Korean fried chicken. I have to change into my swimsuit. This tent behind me is a free changing area, which is super convenient. I'm ready. Let's do this. How's my beach body after an ice cream?

Should I go complain? I guess I just have to eat this. Just kidding, guys, but I did have a subscriber ask how to complain in English, so if you ever get the wrong order, you can say something like, excuse me, I think I got the wrong order, or I'm sorry, but this isn't what I ordered.

title: should (advice)
examples: Should I go complain? I guess I just have to eat this. Just kidding, guys, but I did have a subscriber ask how to complain in English, so if you ever get the wrong order, you can say something like, excuse me, I think I got the wrong order, or I'm sorry, but this isn't what I ordered.

title: had better / it's time
examples: I'm slathering on some sunscreen because it's time to lay out and try to get a tan, and I don't want to get burnt. I like strapless bathing suits like this, so I don't get any weird tan lines.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: This reminds me of the time my husband and I were at a beach in California, and he lost his wedding ring in the sand. We even hired someone with a metal detector to try and help us find it, but we couldn't find it in the end. So if you have jewelry when you're at the beach, be extra careful. I can't leave the beach without writing my name in the sand.

title: Passive (special: get + p.p.)
examples: I will explain what I'm doing along the way and teach you English expressions and vocabulary that you can use in your daily life. I recommend shadowing me to practice your speaking and improve your pronunciation. First things first, let's pack a beach bag. I've got my beach day essentials in front of me. Obviously I need a swimsuit. Sometimes I wear a bikini, but today I'm packing my one piece. I don't want to get a sunburn, so I've got sunscreen and a sun stick for my face. I'm packing two beach towels and a book, so I don't get bored when I'm tanning.

I don't want to jump y'all. At least I'm getting used to the water. Usually, I like to go under, but I did my makeup for the camera today, so no dunking my head this time. When I go under, I always flood my nose and hold my breath. The buis are so far out, but let's not go too deep. Let's stay in the shallow water. This is my happy place. I'm like a mermaid.
